meta.note: English core locale. Macedonian remains the canonical source for WPA terminology.

wpa.institute.title: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication & Security Studies
wpa.doctrine.protocol_absolute: Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute.
wpa.security.public_wording: WPA is building an information security management system aligned with ISO/IEC 27001 principles as part of a pre-certification readiness process.
wpa.translator.notice: Translations are governed by WPA language control. Macedonian is the canonical source.
wpa.translator.missing: Translation is not available. The canonical Macedonian text is displayed.
home.header.title: An institutional school for protocol, diplomacy and public preparedness.
home.header.subtitle: WPA develops educational, research and media infrastructure for protocol, diplomacy, public communication, security studies and communicology.
home.header.note: The platform is in an institutional setup phase. Content, programmes and the journal are being developed transparently, without false claims of accreditation, indexing or certification.
home.hero.card1.title: Education
home.hero.card1.text: Programmes and materials for protocol literacy, institutional conduct and public presence.
home.hero.card2.title: Research
home.hero.card2.text: Publications, analyses and WPA Journal as a gradually developed scholarly infrastructure.
home.hero.card3.title: Practical Application
home.hero.card3.text: Scenarios, laboratories and media tools for real institutional situations.
institute.meta.title: WPA Institute · Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies
institute.topbar.text: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies
institute.hero.title: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies
institute.hero.lead: World Protocol Academy connects academic theory with professional practice, ethics and measurability in the fields of protocol, diplomacy, public communication, security studies and communication skills.
institute.hero.formula_text: Theory + practice + ethics + measurability = a living school of protocol, diplomacy, public communication and security.
institute.tools_hub.label: Public Tools Hub
institute.tools_hub.title: Public Tools of the WPA Institute
institute.tools_hub.lead: Quick access to the Institute's main public functions: WPA Journal, Trust Layer, Index Tools, Student Desk Beta, AI Roadmap, WPAWS, Audio Media Engine and author support.
institute.tools_hub.tag.author: Author Support
institute.tools_hub.master_list.title: Master List v1.0
institute.tools_hub.master_list.text: 125 institutions · 7 comparative groups · 2 reference sections · complete fields. JSON and CSV format.
institute.tools_hub.press.title: Press Release
institute.tools_hub.press.text: Invitation for media collaboration on the WPA Global Institutions Index.
institute.tools_hub.journal.text: Specialised journal for protocol, diplomacy, public communication, security and communicology.
institute.tools_hub.dois.title: WPA Working Papers 001–004
institute.tools_hub.dois.text: Official DOI index for the first four WPA Working Papers, published as separate Zenodo records with PDF, DOCX and README files.
institute.tools_hub.trust.title: Trust & Corrections
institute.tools_hub.trust.text: Public notice, correction request and rights & takedown pathway.
institute.tools_hub.index.title: Index Tools
institute.tools_hub.index.text: Master List, reference analysis methodology and institutional correction process.
institute.tools_hub.student.title: Student Desk Beta
institute.tools_hub.student.text: AI-supported learning space for protocol, diplomacy, communication and security culture.
institute.tools_hub.ai.title: AI Innovation Roadmap
institute.tools_hub.ai.text: AI-assisted video, research and editorial workflows, with content provenance, AI transparency and a human review pathway.
institute.tools_hub.author.title: Author Support & Indexing Readiness
institute.tools_hub.author.text: Ethical manuscript preparation, citation integrity, AI transparency and screening against predatory journals.
institute.identity.label: Institutional Identity
institute.identity.title: World Protocol Academy as a Specialised Institute
institute.identity.subtitle: Specialised Academic and Professional Platform
institute.identity.p1: WPA is a specialised academic and professional platform for research, training, applied practice, certification discipline, analytics and institutional reference models within the Institute's domain.
institute.identity.p2: WPA does not present itself as a university and makes no claim of state accreditation. The Institute develops its own specialised methodology, professional standards and analytical indicators.
institute.identity.p3: The Institute operates as a living school: theory, verifiable sources, real practice and measurable results — without academic overreach and without legal overreach.
institute.charter.label: Institute Charter
institute.charter.title: Six Pillars of the Charter
institute.charter.lead: The Charter articulates the foundational principles under which the Institute operates — without negotiation, without exceptions, without special cases.
institute.charter.p1.title: Academic Integrity
institute.charter.p1.text: All research and educational materials are original works of WPA or properly attributed sources.
institute.charter.p2.title: Professional Measurability
institute.charter.p2.text: Every programme, certificate and analytical indicator has clear criteria, a transparent methodology and verifiable evidence.
institute.charter.p3.title: Ethical Independence
institute.charter.p3.text: WPA does not permit payment for better outcomes, does not perform accreditation and does not confer state recognitions.
institute.charter.p4.title: Linguistic Discipline
institute.charter.p4.text: The Institute does not use words such as partner, accredited or endorsed without a specific written agreement.
institute.charter.p5.title: Transparent Sources
institute.charter.p5.text: Nine-field citation scheme, methodological openness and right of correction for the institutions being measured.
institute.charter.p6.title: Founder's Doctrine
institute.charter.p6.text: Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute. — operational logic of all Institute activities.
institute.domains.label: Institute Domains
institute.domains.title: Six Domains of Specialisation
institute.domains.d1.title: Protocol Studies
institute.domains.d1.text: State, diplomatic, institutional, ceremonial and international protocol.
institute.domains.d2.title: Diplomacy and International Relations
institute.domains.d2.text: Diplomatic practice, international relations, public diplomacy and international law.
institute.domains.d3.title: Public Communication
institute.domains.d3.text: Public appearance, public relations, strategic communication and institutional reputation.
institute.domains.d4.title: Security Studies
institute.domains.d4.text: Security studies, defence diplomacy, crisis procedures and public events.
institute.domains.d5.title: Research and Reference Analysis
institute.domains.d5.text: Working papers, case studies, short-term policy recommendations, indices, methodologies and annual reports.
institute.domains.d6.title: Practical Professional Wisdom
institute.domains.d6.text: WPA values both academic sources and long-standing documented practical experience.
institute.research.label: Research Pillars
institute.research.title: Four Main Research Streams
institute.research.lead: The Institute structures its working papers, monographs, short-term policy recommendations and reference analyses into four integrated research pillars. Each pillar has a clear thematic territory, a specialised methodology and identified outputs.
institute.research.r1.title: Protocol Theory and Practice
institute.research.r1.text: Historical development, comparative analysis, ceremonial logic, precedent, precedence, flag protocol, order of precedence, state symbols.
institute.research.r2.title: Diplomacy and International Relations
institute.research.r2.text: Bilateral relations, multilateral frameworks, public diplomacy, defence diplomacy, conference management, negotiation methods.
institute.research.r3.title: Security and Defence Diplomacy
institute.research.r3.text: Public event security, crisis coordination, VIP protection, incident protocol, protection of diplomatic personnel.
institute.research.r4.title: Public Communication and Institutional Reputation
institute.research.r4.text: Strategic communication, public relations, media relations, crisis communication, digital reputation, institutional narrative.
institute.analytics.label: Analytics and Measurability
institute.analytics.title: Centre for Analytics, Measurability and Institutional Indices
institute.analytics.lead: WPA is developing a specialised analytics centre for tracking, comparing and evaluating institutions, academies, universities, programmes, experts and research activity in the fields of protocol, diplomacy, public communication, public relations, security studies and communication skills.
institute.analytics.measured.title: What will be measured
institute.analytics.measured.i3: programmes, teaching content and certification discipline;
institute.analytics.measured.i4: annual conferences, simulations and applied research;
institute.analytics.measured.i5: practical professional impact and knowledge transfer;
institute.analytics.measured.i6: international cooperation, public communication, digital transparency.
institute.analytics.notwhat.title: What the WPA Index is NOT
institute.analytics.notwhat.i2: is not a university ranking in the classical sense;
institute.analytics.notwhat.i4: does not replace Scopus, Web of Science, ORCID, Google Scholar;
institute.analytics.notwhat.i5: does not allow payment for a better outcome;
institute.analytics.notwhat.i6: does not constitute a final assessment without possibility of correction.
institute.analytics.index.title: Institute Index — four dimensions
institute.analytics.index.d1.title: Research and Publications
institute.analytics.index.d1.text: Working papers, books, articles, citation visibility, ORCID and Google Scholar evidence.
institute.analytics.index.d2.title: Programmes and Certification
institute.analytics.index.d2.text: Programme structure, learning outcomes, assessment discipline, verification.
institute.analytics.index.d3.title: Professional Practice
institute.analytics.index.d3.text: Documented practitioner activity, applied research, real institutional application.
institute.analytics.index.d4.title: Transparency and Cooperation
institute.analytics.index.d4.text: Publication openness, international cooperation, digital institutional clarity.
institute.analytics.index.note: Category weights are indicative, not final. Final weights will be published in the public Institute Index v1.0 methodology after review by the Academic Advisory Board.
institute.analytics.disclaimer: WPA indices are specialised analytical tools. Final assessments are based on publicly verifiable evidence, methodological transparency and, where required, human review by WPA and external advisers.
institute.trust.label: Trust · Provenance · Corrections
institute.trust.title: Trust, Provenance & Correction Layer
institute.trust.lead: WPA uses a public trust layer for transparency, right of correction, takedown requests, AI transparency, content provenance and methodological discipline.
institute.trust.li1: right of correction for institutions and individuals;
institute.trust.li2: public disclaimer for Institute Index and benchmark materials;
institute.trust.li3: AI transparency and human review of content;
institute.trust.li5: clear distinction between analysis, opinion, index and accreditation.
institute.trust.not_li4: is not a paid mechanism for a better outcome;
institute.trust.not_li5: is not a public release of private data.
institute.index.label: WPA Index Tools
institute.index.title: WPA Global Institutions Index and Benchmark Tools
institute.index.lead: Public entry point to the WPA Master List, benchmark methodology and correction process for institutions in the fields of protocol, diplomacy, public communication and security studies.
institute.index.tool1.label: TOOL 1
institute.index.tool1.title: Master List
institute.index.tool1.text: Working list of institutions grouped into fair categories.
institute.index.tool2.label: TOOL 2
institute.index.tool2.title: Reference Analysis
institute.index.tool2.text: A reference map for comparison, not a final ranking.
institute.index.tool3.label: TOOL 3
institute.index.tool3.title: Correction
institute.index.tool3.text: Institutions can request correction of public data.
institute.index.tool4.label: TOOL 4
institute.index.tool4.title: Methodology
institute.index.tool4.text: Methodology with publicly verifiable evidence and transparent categories.
institute.index.note: The WPA Index is not a state accreditation, is not a professional licence and does not replace Scopus, Web of Science, ORCID, Google Scholar or national accreditation bodies.
institute.methodology.label: Methodological Groups
institute.methodology.title: Categories for Fair Comparison
institute.methodology.lead: WPA will not lump small protocol academies together with large universities in a single crude table. Comparison is done by category, with clear criteria for membership in each group.
institute.methodology.gA.name: Protocol and etiquette academies and diplomatic-protocol academies
institute.methodology.gA.examples: Protocol Academy of Macedonia, IEPA London, Accademia del Cerimoniale, Protocol School of Washington
institute.methodology.gB.name: Diplomatic academies and government training institutes
institute.methodology.gB.examples: Diplomatic Academy of Vienna, Bulgarian Diplomatic Institute, Diplomacy Academy Türkiye, Clingendael Institute
institute.methodology.gC.name: Universities and faculties of diplomacy, international relations, law and political sciences
institute.methodology.gC.examples: China Foreign Affairs University, Sciences Po, CEDS France, College of Europe
institute.methodology.gD.name: Foreign service institutes
institute.methodology.gD.examples: US FSI, Instituto Rio Branco, ISEN Argentina, Foreign Service Institute of India, FSI Indonesia
institute.methodology.gE.name: Institutes for cultural and intercultural diplomacy
institute.methodology.gE.examples: Institute for Cultural Diplomacy, Anna Lindh Foundation, British Council Cultural Relations
institute.methodology.gF.name: Training centres of international organisations
institute.methodology.gF.examples: UN Staff College, OSCE Academy, NATO School, EU College of Europe (Bruges/Natolin)
institute.methodology.gG.name: Public relations and communication studies
institute.methodology.gG.examples: USC Annenberg, NYU SPS, University of Amsterdam, NTU Wee Kim Wee School
institute.methodology.gH.name: Security, defence and strategic studies
institute.methodology.gH.examples: NATO Defense College, King's College War Studies, Georgetown SSP, ANU SDSC
institute.methodology.gI.name: Globally prestigious reference institutions
institute.methodology.gI.examples: Georgetown SFS, Johns Hopkins SAIS, Harvard Kennedy School, Oxford, LSE
institute.benchmark.label: Global Benchmark Map
institute.benchmark.title: Global Map of Reference Institutions
institute.benchmark.lead: The map is not a final ranking. It is an initial analytical map of selected institutions serving as reference points for the development of the WPA methodology.
institute.benchmark.r1.title: North America
institute.benchmark.r2.title: Latin America
institute.benchmark.r3.title: Europe
institute.benchmark.r4.title: Protocol Academies
institute.benchmark.r5.title: Asia and the Middle East
institute.benchmark.r6.title: Security and Oceania
institute.benchmark.inst.anu_strategic_a: ANU Strategic and Defence Studies Centre
institute.benchmark.disclaimer: For public use WPA uses only verified names and accurate labels. Names with misspellings, duplicates or incorrect countries will not be published.
institute.opc.title: First International Conference on Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Relations and Security
institute.opc.text1: OPC 2026 (Ohrid Protocol Conference) is the Institute's flagship event — a gathering of academic researchers, protocol officers, diplomats, security experts and communicators from over 17 countries.
institute.opc.text2: The conference connects theory with practice, academic authority with field experience, and regional perspective with global standards.
institute.practitioners.label: Practitioner Lecture Series
institute.practitioners.title: Theory Connected to Practice
institute.practitioners.lead: The Institute invites experienced practitioners to deliver specialised lectures from their field. With written consent, lectures may be recorded and become part of the Institute's educational archive intended for students, attendees, researchers and professionals.
institute.practitioners.c1.title: Protocol and Diplomacy
institute.practitioners.c1.text: Heads of protocol, diplomats, former ambassadors, diplomatic officers, ceremonial masters and experts in state and international events.
institute.practitioners.c2.title: Defence, Security and Military Attachés
institute.practitioners.c2.text: Defence and military attachés, security coordinators, experts in public events, crisis procedures, institutional security and inter-institutional coordination.
institute.practitioners.c3.title: Public Communication, Media and Public Relations
institute.practitioners.c3.text: Spokespersons, public-relations directors, journalists, editors, media advisers and experts in crisis communication, public appearance and institutional reputation.
institute.practitioners.c4.title: Visual Diplomacy and Documentation
institute.practitioners.c4.text: Official photographers, camera crews, visual documentarians, translators, interpreters and professionals who shape the public image of diplomatic and protocol events.
institute.practitioners.pathway.title: Practitioner Pathway — Five Steps
institute.practitioners.pathway.s1.title: Invitation
institute.practitioners.pathway.s1.text: The Institute identifies an experienced practitioner based on a publicly documented position and role.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s2.title: Agreement
institute.practitioners.pathway.s2.text: A written consent is signed for the topic, scope and the right to record and archive.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s3.title: Lecture
institute.practitioners.pathway.s3.text: Lecture in a structured format: 45 to 60 minutes of delivery and 15 minutes for questions and answers with the participants.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s4.title: Archive
institute.practitioners.pathway.s4.text: The lecture is added to the WPA educational archive with clear attribution and metadata.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s5.title: Recognition
institute.practitioners.pathway.s5.text: The practitioner receives recognition from the Institute for a documented professional contribution — without an academic title.
institute.practitioners.disclaimer: Participating as a practitioner-lecturer at the Institute does not constitute an academic title, state accreditation or professional licence. It is recognition of documented professional experience and contribution to the Institute's educational mission.
institute.aab.label: Academic Advisory Board
institute.aab.title: AAB — Independent External Advisory
institute.aab.subtitle: Structure and Role
institute.aab.p1: The Academic Advisory Board (AAB) is an independent advisory body composed of distinguished academic and professional figures in the Institute's domain: professors, former diplomats, heads of protocol, security experts and communication specialists.
institute.aab.p2: The AAB reviews the Institute Index methodology, checks the quality of working papers, advises on research priorities and provides an independent opinion on cases involving potential conflicts of interest.
institute.aab.p3: The AAB is not an operational body — the Institute is led by the Director. The AAB is the advisory conscience: a check on quality, not on operational decisions.
institute.author.label: Author Support · Indexing Readiness
institute.author.title: WPA Author Support & Indexing Readiness Desk
institute.author.lead: WPA provides ethical support for authors, researchers and practitioners: manuscript preparation, methodological review, citation readiness, AI transparency, journal selection ethics, predatory journal screening and an understanding of the Scopus, Web of Science, DOAJ, ORCID, Google Scholar, Crossref, ISSN, ISBN and DOI ecosystems.
institute.author.li2: improvement of abstract, title and keywords;
institute.author.li5: ORCID, Google Scholar and ResearchGate readiness;
institute.author.not_li1: does not guarantee publication in Scopus or Web of Science;
institute.author.not_li3: does not sell faster peer review;
institute.author.not_li4: does not manipulate citations or h-index;
institute.author.not_li6: does not claim indexing without official confirmation.
institute.author.note: WPA does not guarantee publication in Scopus, Web of Science, DOAJ or any external index. WPA provides ethical author support, manuscript readiness, methodological review, citation integrity, AI transparency, predatory journal screening and guidance for legitimate academic publishing.
institute.journal_section.label: WPA Journal · Coming Soon
institute.journal_section.title: WPA Journal of Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication, Security & Communicology
institute.journal_section.lead: A specialised international interdisciplinary journal of World Protocol Academy on protocol, diplomacy, public communication, public relations, security studies and communicology.
institute.journal.note: Submission is free. Peer review is free for the author. A symbolic fee applies only upon acceptance, with waivers available. There is no payment for acceptance, no paid ranking and no guaranteed publication.
institute.publications.label: Public Outputs
institute.publications.title: Institute Publications and Analytical Outputs
institute.publications.s0.title: WPA Journal
institute.publications.s0.text: A specialised journal for protocol, diplomacy, public communication, security studies and communicology. The inaugural issue will be released in September 2026; the call for the second issue is open until 31 December 2026.
institute.publications.s1.title: WPA Working Papers
institute.publications.s1.text: The first four WPA Working Papers are published as public Zenodo DOI records: WP-001, WP-002, WP-003 and WP-004.
institute.publications.s2.title: WPA Protocol Studies Review
institute.publications.s2.text: Specialised analyses from the field of protocol and diplomatic practice.
institute.publications.s3.title: WPA Policy Briefs
institute.publications.s3.text: Short applied recommendations for institutions and professionals.
institute.publications.s4.title: WPA Case Studies
institute.publications.s4.text: Documented case studies from the Institute's domain.
institute.publications.s5.title: WPA Annual Report
institute.publications.s5.text: Annual institutional review of activities, results and future directions.
institute.publications.s6.title: WPA Global Benchmark
institute.publications.s6.text: Specialised benchmark for protocol, diplomacy, communication and security.
institute.student_section.label: Student Desk Preview · Beta
institute.student.title: AI-supported Learning Space
institute.student.lead: WPA Student Desk Beta is an institutional learning space for students, attendees and researchers in the fields of protocol, diplomacy, public communication, public relations, security studies and communicology.
institute.student.c1.label: Learning
institute.student.c1.text: Structured materials, guided questions and educational scenarios supported by human review.
institute.student.c2.label: Research
institute.student.c2.title: Research and Preparation
institute.student.c2.text: Support for paper preparation, literature search, methodology and submission readiness.
institute.student.c3.label: Discussion
institute.student.c3.title: Institutional Dialogue
institute.student.c3.text: Space for questions, discussions and interaction with mentors and practitioners within a clear institutional framework.
institute.student.note: The Student Desk is in beta. Content is being developed gradually, with human review and without false claims of accreditation or certification.
institute.ai_section.label: AI Innovation · Media · Provenance
institute.ai.title: WPA AI Innovation & Media Roadmap
institute.ai.lead: World Protocol Academy tracks the latest AI tools for video, audio, translation, educational automation, content verification, provenance and public communication. These technologies are integrated carefully, with human review, AI transparency and institutional responsibility.
institute.ai.c1.label: Video AI
institute.ai.c1.title: Video AI Workflow
institute.ai.c1.text: WPA will use AI video generation and video-editing tools to support educational scenarios, public communication, course previews, protocol simulations and short explanatory videos — always under human control and with clear labelling.
institute.ai.c2.label: Research AI
institute.ai.c2.title: Research AI Workflow
institute.ai.c2.text: Advanced reasoning and long-form drafting models are used to prepare drafts, structures, research maps, comparative analyses and working documents. Final responsibility always remains human and editorial.
institute.ai.c3.label: Audio Media
institute.ai.c3.title: Audio & Voice Production
institute.ai.c3.text: AI audio tools can help prepare voiceovers, audiobook chapters, lecture narration, multilingual summaries and training materials, with respect for consent, attribution and institutional dignity.
institute.ai.c4.title: Content Provenance & Trust Layer
institute.ai.c4.text: WPA will develop a trust layer for AI-generated or AI-assisted content via provenance records, disclosure notes, content credentials and correction pathways, without misrepresenting the origin of content.
institute.ai.c5.label: WPAWS
institute.ai.c5.title: AI Agent Automation
institute.ai.c5.text: WPAWS will be developed as an institutional workspace for ideas, papers, books, courses, presentations, videos, journal submissions, index workflows and public communication packages.
institute.ai.c6.label: Ethics
institute.ai.c6.title: Human Review & AI Transparency
institute.ai.c6.text: AI may assist in the process, but must not fabricate facts, citations, consents, institutional ties or academic status. Every public piece of content goes through human review.
institute.ai.note: WPA does not claim that AI systems are infallible, nor that AI can replace academic, editorial or institutional responsibility. AI is used as a supporting tool, not as a standalone authority. All sensitive content, public statements, index assessments, certificates and journal decisions require human review.
institute.cta.title: The Institute is building a serious, modern, internationally oriented academy — with structure, dignity and clear direction.
institute.footer.about_text: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies. Institutional formula: theory and practice, ethics and measurability.
institute.footer.bottom: © 2026 World Protocol Academy · Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies · Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute.
